title: Goose Tales
description:
**Goose Tales** is an _in-progress_ browser-based MMO inspired by the social experiences of classic Flash games. Built with [Pixi.js](https://pixijs.com), [Express](https://expressjs.com/en/), and [Socket.io](https://socket.io), the project aims to recreate the accessibility of early web games while leveraging modern web technologies and a custom multiplayer architecture. Rather than relying on traditional 2D artwork, character assets are first modeled in 3D and converted into prerendered sprite sheets through a custom rendering pipeline, following the same techniques used by many classic games to achieve detailed visuals with lightweight rendering. This approach combines the flexibility of 3D asset creation with the performance and aesthetic of classic 2D sprites. ![Goose Tales Demo](/assets/demo/goose-tales-demo-1.gif) The application renders a real-time 2D world using Pixi.js and employs an authoritative server model to manage player connections, entity state, and multiplayer interactions. Communication between clients and the server is performed through WebSockets, enabling real-time synchronization of player movement, animations, and world events across all connected users.

Beyond its technology stack, Goose Tales focuses on the engineering challenges of massively multiplayer browser games, including custom networking protocols, entity synchronization, event-driven systems, asset pipelines, and scalable game state management. By combining modern frontend technologies with custom multiplayer systems, the project aims to deliver a persistent, lightweight MMO experience that captures the spirit of classic Flash-era games while remaining entirely accessible from the browser.

title: Go → x86 Compiler
description:
**Go → x86 Compiler** is a compiler written in **C++** that translates a subset of the [Go](https://go.dev/) language into **x86 assembly**.

The program parses modified Go source code and generates usable x86 assembly output. It supports core language features such as variable declarations, arithmetic and relational operations, conditional statements, and function definitions and calls.

Beyond the basic subset, the compiler also implements support for structs, arrays (including multidimensional arrays), strings, and ternary operations. The project focuses on the full compilation pipeline: lexical analysis, parsing, semantic validation, and code generation, targeting low-level assembly as the final output.

title: VR Band
description:
**VR Band** is a collaborative virtual reality experience for **Meta Quest**, developed in [Unity](https://unity.com/) using the [Meta XR SDK](https://developers.meta.com/horizon/documentation/unity/unity-sdks-overview/). The application enables real-time multiplayer jam sessions in VR. Players can join the same local session and perform together using a set of fully interactive instruments: drums, keyboard, xylophone, maracas, and a Peruvian cajón. Each instrument is designed to respond naturally to VR controller input, simulating physical interaction within the virtual space.

Multiplayer synchronization allows participants to share the same environment and play simultaneously, turning the experience into a virtual rehearsal room built specifically for immersive performance.

title: Bones, Camera, Action!
description:
**Bones, Camera, Action!** is a web-based motion capture application built with [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/), [Three.js](https://threejs.org/) (via [React Three Fiber](https://r3f.docs.pmnd.rs/)), and [MediaPipe](https://ai.google.dev/edge/mediapipe/solutions/guide).

The application captures the user's pose in real time using MediaPipe's Pose Landmarker. Landmark data is streamed directly into the Three.js scene, where each tracked joint is mapped to a corresponding bone in a 3D rig. Movements are applied live to a pre-rigged character mesh, effectively turning the browser into a simple motion capture system.

The 3D model must be rigged using [Mixamo](https://www.mixamo.com/), as the bone mapping relies on specific naming conventions. By leveraging MediaPipe for pose estimation, the system requires no external sensors or markers, making it a fully browser-based mocap solution.

![Bones, Camera, Action! Demo](/assets/demo/bones-camera-action-demo.gif)

title: Poke-Maps
description:
**Poke-Maps** is a location-based mobile application built with **React Native**. It integrates [react-native-maps](https://github.com/react-native-maps/react-native-maps) for map rendering and consumes data from the [PokeAPI](https://pokeapi.co/) REST service.

The app generates Pokémon markers dynamically around the user's current location. Users can tap on markers to simulate capturing Pokémon, which are then persisted locally on the device using local storage.

![Poke-Maps Demo](/assets/demo/poke-maps-demo.webp)
